evidence.scientific_clarity: Both ROC and PRC panels present for all 4 classifiers; diagonal random-chance line and prevalence baseline (p=0.40) included; AUROC and AP annotated per method in legend as brief requires.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 is absent from gen_figure.py; pdffonts confirms fonts are Type 3 (not Type 42/TrueType); default DejaVu Sans is used throughout, which is the matplotlib default the rubric flags.
evidence.palette: Uses a 4-color Paul Tol-inspired colorblind-safe palette (#4477AA, #EE6677, #228833, #AA3377); no green/red reserved-direction confusion; all four classifiers are distinguishable.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(9,4.2) is reasonable; shared legend placed below with bbox_to_anchor=(0.5,-0.08) and tight_layout; axes span full 0-1 range; minor concern that legend is slightly outside figure frame requiring bbox_inches='tight' to capture.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; legend is shared and placed below both panels; concrete finding ('OursModel achieves highest AUROC and AP') is stated; reference lines are explained with numerical prevalence value.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists but fonts are embedded as Type 3 (confirmed via pdffonts); pdf.fonttype=42 is not set in the script, so text is not stored as selectable TrueType outlines.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial (PDF 24 kB, PNG 107 kB, caption 481 B, script 4.3 kB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains both panels, identifies reference lines (diagonal = random chance; horizontal = prevalence p=0.40), notes legend reports AUROC and AP, and explicitly names OursModel as the best performer — a specific, quantitative finding.

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe palette with good hue separation across all four classifiers; meets brief requirements for AUROC/AP annotation in legend.
Caption is complete and informative: explains reference lines with numerical values, names the winning method, and matches the figure content exactly.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is not set and fonts are Type 3 in the PDF, meaning text is not selectable/editable vector text — a common journal requirement that is unmet.

Default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout; no explicit font configuration in the script, and the legend font size (8.5 pt) is small enough that it may be hard to read in a two-column journal layout.
